title: Bump Buildah to v1.17.0
body:
Bump Buildah to v1.17.0 on Master.

Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *TomSweeneyRedHat* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [TomSweeneyRedHat] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@vrothberg @nalind PTAL.
I vendored this a couple of different times following: https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/install.md#vendoring
and I expected to see changes for at least https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/2718/commits/2a9435428890e3970ecbc3e424fbe74fdd5bbe1f
But no changes popped other that go.mods and modules.txt... 
Is this OK, or did I mess something up?
Looks like you need to do make vendor-in-container.
There's a `replace` way down at the bottom of `go.mod` that's interfering with it.

Could we wait for short names?

On Fri 30 Oct 2020 at 16:54, Nalin Dahyabhai <notifications@github.com>
wrote:

> There's a replace way down at the bottom of go.mod that's interfering
> with it.
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you were mentioned.
>
>
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/8202#issuecomment-719636675>,
> or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/ACZDRAZ4FYJ7YQF62O5UUP3SNLOUNANCNFSM4TFD2DKQ>
> .
>
@vrothberg Let's get podman2.2 into testing, and then you can update buildah next week.  And bump podman version then.
Missed that, TYVM @nalind .  Just removed and re-vendoring.
The previous version (3ac12986e8cfa1d7bc1af4d0549b7f128dc676a3) was just a few commits behind the v1.17.0 tag, so it won't be a big patch.
@nalind just re-pushed and checked.  You're right, small patch, and from what I can see it has the missing pieces in it now.

> @vrothberg Let's get podman2.2 into testing, and then you can update buildah next week. And bump podman version then.

Can't we get Podman v2.2 into testing without cutting a new Buildah? If we don't wait for short names, we had to cut Buildah v1.18 next week.

> > @vrothberg Let's get podman2.2 into testing, and then you can update buildah next week. And bump podman version then.
> 
> Can't we get Podman v2.2 into testing without cutting a new Buildah? If we don't wait for short names, we had to cut Buildah v1.18 next week.

Oh, I thought this was a Buildah PR. It seems this ship has sailed already. I still think this was rushed.
